title: Distinct Subsequences| (DP-32)
content:
Problem Statement : Given two strings s and t, return the number of distinct subsequences of s that equal t.

A subsequence of a string is a new string generated from the original string with some characters (can be none) deleted without changing the relative order of the remaining characters. For example, "ace" is a subsequence of "abcde" while "aec" is not.

The task is to count how many different ways we can form t from s by deleting some (or no) characters from s.

Examples 
Input: s = "axbxax", t = "axa"
Output: 2
Explanation: In the string "axbxax", there are two distinct subsequences "axa":
(a)(x)bx(a)x
(a)xb(x)(a)x 


Input: s = "babgbag", t = "bag"
Output: 5
Explanation: In the string "babgbag", there are five distinct subsequences "bag":
(ba)(b)(ga)(g)
(ba)(bg)(ag)
(bab)(ga)(g)
(bab)(g)(ag)
(babg)(a)(g)

Disclaimer: Here is the practice link to help you assess your knowledge better. It's highly recommend trying to solve it before looking at the solution.

Memoization
Algorithm
The problem asks us to count how many ways we can form string t from string s by deleting characters. Whenever we compare characters from s and t, we have two choices, if the characters match, we can either use it (move both pointers) or skip it (move only in s). If the characters don’t match, the only option is to skip the character from s. This naturally gives us overlapping subproblems, so memoization works perfectly.
  • Define a recursive function f(i, j) which counts ways to form substring of t starting at j from substring of s starting at i
  • If j equals length of t, return 1 because we have successfully formed t
  • If i equals length of s but j is smaller than length of t, return 0 because s is exhausted
  • If s[i] is equal to t[j], then we can either take this character or skip it
  • If characters match, f(i, j) becomes f(i+1, j+1) plus f(i+1, j)
  • If characters do not match, we can only skip the character from s so f(i, j) becomes f(i+1, j)
  • Store results of each state in a 2D memo table dp[i][j] to avoid recomputation
  • Start recursion with f(0, 0) and return the final answer
Code
1234567891011121314151617181920212223242526272829303132333435363738394041424344454647484950515253545556575859606162
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    // Recursive helper function with memoization
    // i -> current index in s
    // j -> current index in t
    // dp -> memoization table
    int helper(int i, int j, string &s, string &t,
               vector<vector<int>> &dp) {
        // If we have matched all characters of t
        // then we found one valid subsequence
        if (j == t.size()) return 1;

        // If we have exhausted s but not t
        // then no valid subsequence can be formed
        if (i == s.size()) return 0;

        // If this state has been already computed
        if (dp[i][j] != -1) return dp[i][j];

        // If current characters of s and t match
        if (s[i] == t[j]) {
            // Option 1: take this character and move both indices
            int take = helper(i + 1, j + 1, s, t, dp);

            // Option 2: skip this character of s and move only in s
            int notTake = helper(i + 1, j, s, t, dp);

            // Store result in dp table
            return dp[i][j] = take + notTake;
        } else {
            // If characters do not match, we can only skip s[i]
            return dp[i][j] = helper(i + 1, j, s, t, dp);
        }
    }

    // Main function to count distinct subsequences
    int numDistinct(string s, string t) {
        // Initialize dp table with -1 (uncomputed states)
        vector<vector<int>> dp(s.size(),
                               vector<int>(t.size(), -1));

        // Start recursion from index 0 of s and t
        return helper(0, 0, s, t, dp);
    }
};

// Driver code
int main() {
    Solution sol;

    // Input strings
    string s = "babgbag";
    string t = "bag";

    // Print number of distinct subsequences
    cout << sol.numDistinct(s, t) << endl;
    return 0;
}

import java.util.*;

class Solution {
    // Recursive helper function with memoization
    int helper(int i, int j, String s, String t,
               int[][] dp) {
        // If we have matched all characters of t
        if (j == t.length()) return 1;

        // If we have exhausted s but not t
        if (i == s.length()) return 0;

        // If already computed
        if (dp[i][j] != -1) return dp[i][j];

        // If characters match
        if (s.charAt(i) == t.charAt(j)) {
            // Option 1: take current character
            int take = helper(i + 1, j + 1, s, t, dp);

            // Option 2: skip current character of s
            int notTake = helper(i + 1, j, s, t, dp);

            // Store result
            dp[i][j] = take + notTake;
        } else {
            // If mismatch, skip character of s
            dp[i][j] = helper(i + 1, j, s, t, dp);
        }
        return dp[i][j];
    }

    // Main function to count distinct subsequences
    public int numDistinct(String s, String t) {
        // Initialize dp array with -1
        int[][] dp = new int[s.length()][t.length()];
        for (int[] row : dp) Arrays.fill(row, -1);

        // Start recursion
        return helper(0, 0, s, t, dp);
    }
}

// Driver class
public class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        Solution sol = new Solution();

        // Input strings
        String s = "babgbag";
        String t = "bag";

        // Print answer
        System.out.println(sol.numDistinct(s, t));
    }
}

class Solution:
    # Recursive helper with memoization
    def helper(self, i, j, s, t, dp):
        # If we have matched all characters of t
        if j == len(t):
            return 1

        # If we have exhausted s but not t
        if i == len(s):
            return 0

        # If result already computed
        if dp[i][j] != -1:
            return dp[i][j]

        # If characters match
        if s[i] == t[j]:
            # Option 1: take the character
            take = self.helper(i + 1, j + 1, s, t, dp)

            # Option 2: skip the character from s
            notTake = self.helper(i + 1, j, s, t, dp)

            # Store result
            dp[i][j] = take + notTake
        else:
            # If mismatch, skip character of s
            dp[i][j] = self.helper(i + 1, j, s, t, dp)

        return dp[i][j]

    # Main function to count distinct subsequences
    def numDistinct(self, s: str, t: str) -> int:
        # Initialize dp table with -1
        dp = [[-1] * len(t) for _ in range(len(s))]

        # Start recursion
        return self.helper(0, 0, s, t, dp)


# Driver code
if __name__ == "__main__":
    sol = Solution()

    # Input strings
    s = "babgbag"
    t = "bag"

    # Print result
    print(sol.numDistinct(s, t))

class Solution {
    // Recursive helper function with memoization
    helper(i, j, s, t, dp) {
        // If we have matched all characters of t
        if (j === t.length) return 1;

        // If we have exhausted s but not t
        if (i === s.length) return 0;

        // If already computed
        if (dp[i][j] !== -1) return dp[i][j];

        // If characters match
        if (s[i] === t[j]) {
            // Option 1: take current character
            let take = this.helper(i + 1, j + 1, s, t, dp);

            // Option 2: skip current character of s
            let notTake = this.helper(i + 1, j, s, t, dp);

            // Store result
            dp[i][j] = take + notTake;
        } else {
            // If mismatch, skip character of s
            dp[i][j] = this.helper(i + 1, j, s, t, dp);
        }
        return dp[i][j];
    }

    // Main function to count distinct subsequences
    numDistinct(s, t) {
        // Initialize dp array with -1
        let dp = Array.from({ length: s.length },
            () => new Array(t.length).fill(-1));

        // Start recursion
        return this.helper(0, 0, s, t, dp);
    }
}

// Driver code
let sol = new Solution();

// Input strings
let s = "babgbag";
let t = "bag";

// Print result
console.log(sol.numDistinct(s, t));
Complexity Analysis Time Complexity: O(N * M), each state defined by character of string s and character of string t is computed once.
Space Complexity: O(N*M) + O(N), extra space is used for storing the dp array and recursion stack space.

Tabulation
Algorithm
In memoization, we solve the problem top-down by making recursive calls and storing results in a dp table to avoid recomputation. To convert it into tabulation, we flip the process. Instead of recursion, we directly fill the dp table bottom-up. Start with the base cases (empty string cases) and build solutions for larger substrings step by step. This avoids recursion stack overhead and makes the solution more efficient.
  • Create a 2D dp table of size (n+1) x (m+1) where n is length of s and m is length of t
  • dp[i][j] will represent number of ways to form t[j:] from s[i:]
  • Initialize dp[n][m] = 1 because empty t can be formed from empty s in one way
  • Initialize dp[i][m] = 1 for all i from 0 to n because empty t can always be formed from any suffix of s
  • Initialize dp[n][j] = 0 for all j less than m because non empty t cannot be formed from empty s
  • Fill the table in reverse order starting from i = n-1 to 0 and j = m-1 to 0
  • If s[i] equals t[j], then dp[i][j] = dp[i+1][j+1] + dp[i+1][j]
  • If s[i] does not equal t[j], then dp[i][j] = dp[i+1][j]
  • Final answer will be stored in dp[0][0]
Code
12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031323334353637383940414243444546
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    // Function to count distinct subsequences
    int numDistinct(string s, string t) {
        // Get lengths of both strings
        int n = s.size();
        int m = t.size();

        // Create dp table of size (n+1) x (m+1)
        vector<vector<long long>> dp(n + 1, vector<long long>(m + 1, 0));

        // Base case: empty t can be formed from any suffix of s
        for (int i = 0; i <= n; i++) {
            dp[i][m] = 1;
        }

        // Fill dp table from bottom to top
        for (int i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
            for (int j = m - 1; j >= 0; j--) {
                // If characters match, we have two options:
                // 1. Take this character -> dp[i+1][j+1]
                // 2. Skip this character -> dp[i+1][j]
                if (s[i] == t[j]) {
                    dp[i][j] = dp[i + 1][j + 1] + dp[i + 1][j];
                } else {
                    // If characters don't match, we can only skip
                    dp[i][j] = dp[i + 1][j];
                }
            }
        }

        // Final answer is stored at dp[0][0]
        return dp[0][0];
    }
};

int main() {
    Solution sol;
    string s = "babgbag";
    string t = "bag";
    cout << sol.numDistinct(s, t) << endl; 
    return 0;
}

import java.util.*;

class Solution {
    // Function to count distinct subsequences
    public int numDistinct(String s, String t) {
        // Get lengths of both strings
        int n = s.length();
        int m = t.length();

        // Create dp table of size (n+1) x (m+1)
        long[][] dp = new long[n + 1][m + 1];

        // Base case: empty t can be formed from any suffix of s
        for (int i = 0; i <= n; i++) {
            dp[i][m] = 1;
        }

        // Fill dp table from bottom to top
        for (int i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
            for (int j = m - 1; j >= 0; j--) {
                // If characters match, we have two options:
                // 1. Take this character -> dp[i+1][j+1]
                // 2. Skip this character -> dp[i+1][j]
                if (s.charAt(i) == t.charAt(j)) {
                    dp[i][j] = dp[i + 1][j + 1] + dp[i + 1][j];
                } else {
                    // If characters don't match, we can only skip
                    dp[i][j] = dp[i + 1][j];
                }
            }
        }

        // Final answer is stored at dp[0][0]
        return (int) dp[0][0];
    }
}

public class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        Solution sol = new Solution();
        String s = "babgbag";
        String t = "bag";
        System.out.println(sol.numDistinct(s, t)); 
    }
}

class Solution:
    # Function to count distinct subsequences
    def numDistinct(self, s: str, t: str) -> int:
        # Get lengths of both strings
        n = len(s)
        m = len(t)

        # Create dp table of size (n+1) x (m+1) initialized to 0
        dp = [[0] * (m + 1) for _ in range(n + 1)]

        # Base case: empty t can be formed from any suffix of s
        for i in range(n + 1):
            dp[i][m] = 1

        # Fill dp table from bottom to top
        for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
            for j in range(m - 1, -1, -1):
                # If characters match, we have two options:
                # 1. Take this character -> dp[i+1][j+1]
                # 2. Skip this character -> dp[i+1][j]
                if s[i] == t[j]:
                    dp[i][j] = dp[i + 1][j + 1] + dp[i + 1][j]
                else:
                    # If characters don't match, we can only skip
                    dp[i][j] = dp[i + 1][j]

        # Final answer is stored at dp[0][0]
        return dp[0][0]

class Solution {
    // Function to count distinct subsequences
    numDistinct(s, t) {
        // Get lengths of both strings
        const n = s.length;
        const m = t.length;

        // Create dp table of size (n+1) x (m+1) filled with 0
        const dp = Array.from({ length: n + 1 }, () => Array(m + 1).fill(0));

        // Base case: empty t can be formed from any suffix of s
        for (let i = 0; i <= n; i++) {
            dp[i][m] = 1;
        }

        // Fill dp table from bottom to top
        for (let i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
            for (let j = m - 1; j >= 0; j--) {
                // If characters match, we have two options:
                // 1. Take this character -> dp[i+1][j+1]
                // 2. Skip this character -> dp[i+1][j]
                if (s[i] === t[j]) {
                    dp[i][j] = dp[i + 1][j + 1] + dp[i + 1][j];
                } else {
                    // If characters don't match, we can only skip
                    dp[i][j] = dp[i + 1][j];
                }
            }
        }

        // Final answer is stored at dp[0][0]
        return dp[0][0];
    }
}

// Driver code
const s = "babgbag";
const t = "bag";
const sol = new Solution();
console.log(sol.numDistinct(s, t));
Complexity Analysis Time Complexity: O(N * M), each state defined by character of string s and character of string t is computed once.
Space Complexity: O(N*M) , extra space is used for storing the dp array.

Space Optimization
Algorithm
In tabulation, we use a 2D dp table of size (n+1) x (m+1) to store results. But if we look closely, to compute the current row dp[i][*], we only need the next row dp[i+1][*]. This means we don’t need the entire table but just two 1D arrays to find the number of distinct subsequences.
  • Create a 1D dp array of size m+1 where m is the length of t
  • Initialize dp[m] = 1 because empty t can be formed from any suffix of s
  • Fill the array backwards from i = n-1 to 0 where n is the length of s
  • For each character in s, create a copy of dp to represent current row
  • Iterate j from m-1 down to 0 to avoid overwriting required values
  • If s[i] equals t[j], then dp[j] = previous[j+1] + previous[j]
  • If s[i] does not equal t[j], then dp[j] = previous[j]
  • At the end, dp[0] will store the total number of distinct subsequences
Code
12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031323334353637383940414243444546474849505152
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    // Function to count distinct subsequences
    int numDistinct(string s, string t) {
        // Length of source string
        int n = s.size();
        // Length of target string
        int m = t.size();

        // Create dp array of size m+1, initialize with 0
        vector<long long> dp(m + 1, 0);

        // Base case: Empty target string can always be formed
        // in exactly 1 way (by deleting all characters of s)
        dp[m] = 1;

        // Traverse source string from end to start
        for (int i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
            // Create a copy of dp for current iteration
            vector<long long> curr = dp;

            // Traverse target string from end to start
            for (int j = m - 1; j >= 0; j--) {
                // If characters match, we can either take it or skip it
                if (s[i] == t[j]) {
                    curr[j] = dp[j + 1] + dp[j];
                } 
                // Else we can only skip this character of s
                else {
                    curr[j] = dp[j];
                }
            }

            // Update dp for next iteration
            dp = curr;
        }

        // Answer is number of ways to form entire t
        return (int)dp[0];
    }
};

int main() {
    Solution sol;
    string s = "rabbbit";
    string t = "rabbit";
    cout << sol.numDistinct(s, t) << endl; 
    return 0;
}

import java.util.*;

class Solution {
    // Function to count distinct subsequences
    public int numDistinct(String s, String t) {
        // Length of source string
        int n = s.length();
        // Length of target string
        int m = t.length();

        // Create dp array of size m+1
        long[] dp = new long[m + 1];

        // Base case: Empty target string can always be formed
        dp[m] = 1;

        // Traverse source string from end to start
        for (int i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
            // Create copy for current iteration
            long[] curr = Arrays.copyOf(dp, m + 1);

            // Traverse target string from end to start
            for (int j = m - 1; j >= 0; j--) {
                // If characters match, take it or skip it
                if (s.charAt(i) == t.charAt(j)) {
                    curr[j] = dp[j + 1] + dp[j];
                } 
                // Else only skip option
                else {
                    curr[j] = dp[j];
                }
            }

            // Update dp
            dp = curr;
        }

        // Return answer
        return (int)dp[0];
    }
}

class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        Solution sol = new Solution();
        String s = "rabbbit";
        String t = "rabbit";
        System.out.println(sol.numDistinct(s, t));
    }
}

class Solution:
    # Function to count distinct subsequences
    def numDistinct(self, s: str, t: str) -> int:
        # Length of source string
        n = len(s)
        # Length of target string
        m = len(t)

        # Create dp array of size m+1 initialized to 0
        dp = [0] * (m + 1)

        # Base case: Empty target can always be formed
        dp[m] = 1

        # Traverse source string backwards
        for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
            # Create a copy of dp for current iteration
            curr = dp[:]

            # Traverse target string backwards
            for j in range(m - 1, -1, -1):
                # If characters match, we can take or skip
                if s[i] == t[j]:
                    curr[j] = dp[j + 1] + dp[j]
                # Else only skip option
                else:
                    curr[j] = dp[j]

            # Update dp
            dp = curr

        # Answer is number of ways to form entire t
        return dp[0]


# Driver code

s = "rabbbit"
t = "rabbit"
print(sol.numDistinct(s, t))

class Solution {
    // Function to count distinct subsequences
    numDistinct(s, t) {
        // Length of source string
        let n = s.length;
        // Length of target string
        let m = t.length;

        // Create dp array of size m+1
        let dp = new Array(m + 1).fill(0);

        // Base case: Empty target can always be formed
        dp[m] = 1;

        // Traverse source string backwards
        for (let i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
            // Copy dp for current iteration
            let curr = [...dp];

            // Traverse target string backwards
            for (let j = m - 1; j >= 0; j--) {
                // If characters match, take or skip
                if (s[i] === t[j]) {
                    curr[j] = dp[j + 1] + dp[j];
                } 
                // Else only skip option
                else {
                    curr[j] = dp[j];
                }
            }

            // Update dp
            dp = curr;
        }

        // Return answer
        return dp[0];
    }
}

// Driver code
let sol = new Solution();
let s = "rabbbit";
let t = "rabbit";
console.log(sol.numDistinct(s, t));
Complexity Analysis Time Complexity: O(N * M), each state defined by character of string s and character of string t is computed once.
Space Complexity: O(M) , extra space is used for storing the 1D array.
